i-am-the-equation-mqzt2lqk.mp3.title: I Am the Equation
i-am-the-equation-mqzt2lqk.mp3.lyrics:
I am the equation that bends the light,
Born in the patent office, burning bright,
They say I proved the world is strange and curved,
That energy and mass are intertwined, observed.

I am the search for what holds all things,
The unified dream that my restless mind sings,
In Switzerland, then Berlin, then exile's call,
I tried to answer: what connects it all?

E equals mc squared--I gave them that,
A whisper that became the sound of fact,
But then they took my words and made them burn,
I watched the cities ash and had to turn away.

I am the conscience caught between two worlds,
The scientist whose equations became unfurled
Into the hands of those who loved the dark,
Who lit the sky with my own stolen spark.

They ask me now: did you know what you'd done?
I say: I only sought to understand the sun,
To feel the pulse beneath reality's skin,
To ask the questions that let truth slip in.

I am still searching in this distant shore,
For what I couldn't find before,
A formula for peace, a law of grace,
Some equation written on the human face.

I am the question, not the final word,
The violin note that trembles, unheard,
In chambers where the silence speaks the most,
I am the exile, the uncertain ghost.

But listen: in the bending of the light,
In every child who learns and wonders right,
In every mind that dares to ask "what if?"--
I am alive, I am the gift.
